order, ensuring that the annual physical count of all properties of the

submitted to the Audit Team not later than January 31, of each year.

be the Chairman of the Inventory Committee.

1.12 Lastly, we recommended and Management agreed that the Property Division

respectively, in compliance with Section 114 of the Manual in NGAS, Volume
I.

ensure the immediate establishment and proper maintenance of Local Road
Network Ledger Cards (LRNLC) for all road networks and their
components, following the format prescribed in Annex D of COA Circular
No. 2015-008, and anchored on a precise baseline established through a
thorough physical inventory of all road networks and their components.

physical inventory and reconciliation of records with the Municipal
Accountant, depreciation expenses be recomputed separately for each road
component. Any necessary adjustments should be reflected in the books of
accounts to ensure compliance with IPSAS 17 and COA Circular No. 2015-
008.

Municipal Engineer adequately and thoroughly plan all projects before their
inclusion in the Annual Investment Plan (AIP), ensure the timely implementation
of priority projects, and closely monitor their full implementation as outlined in
the AIP.

promptly remit all taxes withheld from employees’ compensation,
procurements, and documentary stamps in accordance with the prescribed
period outlined in BIR Revenue Memorandum Circular (RMC) No. 23-2012,
dated February 14, 2012. We further recommended and the Municipal
Accountant agreed to maintain the subsidiary ledgers for each specific
account.

We recommended and Management agreed to implement a review process
that would ensure that the monthly remittance matches the total funds
withheld, including the performance of reconciliation procedures to address
any discrepancies between the withheld amounts and the prepared
remittances, and thereafter ensure the timely remittance of all withheld funds
in compliance with the applicable laws, rules, and regulations governing the
remittances to GSIS, PhilHealth, and Pag-ibig.

documentation and timely dissemination of the approved SEF Budget to the
Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Accountant, and Municipal Treasurer,

Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017.

6.8 We further recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal
Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal School Board (MSB), ensure
the prompt and regular submission of the quarterly and annual SEF
Utilization Reports to the DepED Central Office, the Sangganiang Bayan, and
the DBM and DILG Regional Offices, as mandated by the same Circular.

agreed to refrain from approving the PO unless it contains the required
details of the goods to be procured.

observations.recommendation:
1.14 We recommended and Management agreed to observe the guidelines and
procedures provided in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020, for
the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances to have reliable PPE balances
that are verifiable as to existence, condition, and accountability as follows:

a. Strictly follow the guidelines and procedures in the conduct of physical
count of PPE, Recognition of PPE items found at the station, and
disposition for non-existing/missing PPE items and require the Accountant
and Property Officer to reconcile their records based on the results of the
actual physical inventory and effect necessary adjustments;
b. Adopt the uniform numbering system for property numbers of PPE and
require the Property Unit to update the property stickers based on the
prescribed format and to include the required information under Section
5.6 and 5.7 of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020;
c. Submit the approved PIP to the COA Audit Team within the prescribed
period;
d. Conduct the required preliminary activities prior to inventory taking; and
e. Record/document the physical count daily in a standard ICF prescribed in
Annex A of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

2.12 We recommended that Management exclude regular maintenance activities that
are properly chargeable to the General Fund in the preparation of the annual
investment plan and include in the 20% DF only those PPAs that truly
contribute to the development of the community’s development and that
partake of the nature of investment and capital projects.

2.13 We further recommended that the wages of laborers performing regular repair
and maintenance work be charged to the General Fund.

3.6 We recommended and the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management
Officer (MDRRMO) agreed to coordinate with the MDRRMC in identifying the
PPAs to be charged to the unexpended previous years’ LDRRMF balance for
inclusion in the LDRRMFIP in accordance with Section 5.1.2 of COA Circular
No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

4.7 We recommended and the MDRRMO agreed to prepare the Monthly Report on
Sources and Utilization of DRRMF duly reconciled with the accounting records
and certified correct by the Municipal Accountant and submit it to the Auditor
on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.

5.7 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant revise the Notes to the
Financial Statements to include the principal amount, interest rate, maturity
date, purpose of the loan, and interest expense of the Loans Payable - Domestic
account.

6.11 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
a. Maintain the SLs for the Due to Other NGAs account duly indicating the
pertinent information such as the date when the fund was received, the source
agency, the purpose, and the amount received, utilized, and liquidated;
b. Return the excess funds for the completed projects amounting to ₱158,775.54
to the respective Source Agencies; and
c. Ensure that fund transfers recorded under the Trust Fund are duly supported
by a MOA.

7.15 We recommended and Management agreed to:
a) Direct the Municipal Assessor to coordinate with and seek technical
assistance from the Provincial Assessor and/or the DOF-Bureau of Local
Government Finance to fast-track the preparation of the updated SMVs of
the Municipality pursuant to Section 212 of the LGC and cause the drafting
of an Ordinance for consideration of the Sanggunian, incorporating therein
the proposed SMV, assessment level and tax rate to support the conduct of
GRRPA within the reglementary period;
b) Augment the number of qualified personnel assigned at the Municipal
Assessor’s Office to meet the minimum personnel requirements stipulated in
the MRPAAO and conduct capacity-building interventions to facilitate the
conduct of the GRRPA in adherence to Section 219 of the same Code and
DILG-DOF JMC No. 2010-001, and closely monitor its completion; and
c) Make representation with the Local Chief Executive/Local Sanggunian to
appropriate funds for the computerization of the assessment records to hasten
the completion of GRRPA data gathering and encoding.

8.8 We recommend that the BAC prepare the PMR to be approved by the HOPE
and submit the same to the GPPB in printed and electronic formats within 14
calendar days after the end of each semester in accordance with Section 12.2 of
the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to institutionalize the PSCP by creating a working group that will perform the following activities: a. To conduct a risk assessment b. To determine scenarios that may disrupt normal operation; c. To conduct risk analysis and impact analysis; d. To formulate the PSCP based on the identified risks to critical processes and functions and the related steps to be followed to eliminate, if not mitigate, the impact of the disruptions following the prescribed templates and requirements in the comprehensive PSCP guidebook; e. To submit the plan to the MDRRMO for review, to the Local Chief Executive for approval, and to the Sangguniang Bayan for the passage of a resolution to institutionalize the PSCP.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality had participated in a PSCP Training conducted by the OCD on May 23-26, 2023, at El Suites, Siquijor. The technical working group had already been working on the Comprehensive Land Use Plan, mainstreamed the Climate and Disaster Risk Assessment (CIDRA) framework, and identified the appropriate risk reduction and adaptation and mitigation measures as inputs to the comprehensive development planning and investment programming.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management to: a) Stop the practice of recording purchases of supplies and materials directly to the related expense account and, henceforth, record purchases and utilization of these items in accordance with Sections 114 and 121 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I; b) Require the requisitioning office needing the supplies and materials to prepare and submit the RIS to the Assistant Municipal Treasurer-ICO as the property custodian on a weekly basis; c)Require the AMT-ICO/Property Custodian to consolidate all the RIS on the SSMI and submit the same to the Accounting Office; and d)Require the Municipal Accountant to record to the related expense accounts the supplies consumed based on the SSMI and to maintain perpetual inventory records to be reconciled periodically with the records of the AMT-ICO in order to fairly present the expense and inventory accounts in the financial statements.

Impose liquidated damages equal to one tenth of one percent of the cost of the unperformed portion of the works for every day of delay, which shall be deducted from any money due or which may become due the contractor under the contract and/or collect such liquidated damages from the retention money or other securities posted by the contractor whichever is convenient to the Municipality of Larena. Require the project engineer to comply with the necessary requirements prior to the start of any project to avoid delays in its implementation. Ensure that future projects be implemented in accordance with the purposes they were proposed for thru proper supervision by the project coordinator and that provisions of the agreement should be adhered to.
observations.managementAction: Liquidated damages was collected from the contractor.
observations.reason: Accomplishment to date is 99.15%. Imposition of liquidated damages was done.

observations.recommendation: Direct those who receive the PEI to refund the amount of P9,045.83 each, the payment of which is not legally due to them, the PS limitation provided under Section 325(a) of R.A. 7160, having been not observed by the municipality. Henceforth, strictly observe the fifty-five (55%) limitation on personal services to prevent disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: Repayments were collected through payroll deduction.
observations.reason: Post-audit of payroll confirmed deduction of said disallowance however, limited paying capacity of the persons liable hinders the outright collection of the full refund.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Treasurer to send letters of collections to some debtors who are still in service or still alive. Make representations to the COA through the Sangguniang Bayan for possible writing-off of those accounts which could no longer be collected or of doubtful collectability.
observations.managementAction: Request for write-off has 2008 been submitted of which no reply was received to date.
observations.reason: Request for write-off has long been outstanding hence, a copy of COA Circular 2016-005 re: write off of dormant accounts was given to management.
